zh.lifeSummary: William Perry於1612年出生在英國戴文布藍斯可姆。他於1635年5月25日與Charity Martin在英國戴文布藍斯可姆結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他埋葬在英國戴文布藍斯可姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Perry于1612年出生在英国戴文布藍斯可姆。他于1635年5月25日与Charity Martin在英国戴文布藍斯可姆结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他埋葬在英国戴文布藍斯可姆。

nameMeanings.name: Perry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Herry ‘son of Herry’, a variant of the personal name Harry (see Harris ).

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a pear tree (Middle English piri(e), peri(e), puri(e), Old English pirige, pyrige), or a habitational name for someone who owned or came from a place so named. This surname and a number of variants have been established in Ireland since the 17th century.

variants.name: Peery
meanings: Irish: variant of   Perry  , taken to Ireland at the time of thePlantation of Ulster and in the Cromwellian period.

variants.name: Pear
meanings: English: topographic name from residence by a pear tree (Middle English pere, Old English peru, pere). Compare Perry .
English: nickname from Middle English pere, Old French per, peer ‘peer or paragon’, also ‘companion’.

variants.name: Peary
meanings: History: Robert E. Peary (1856–1920) was a U.S.-born explorer, who in 1909 led the first successful expedition to the North Pole. Peary Land in the north of Greenland is named after him, as he explored there in 1892 and 1900.

variants.name: Perrie
meanings: Scottish: variant of Perry 2. Alternatively, perhaps from the Old French personal name Pierre; see Pierce .

variants.name: Harris
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Perryman
meanings: English (Devon): elaborated form of Perry , with Middle English man, probably denoting one who lived near a pear tree; see Pearman .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: William Perry was born in 1612, in Branscombe, Devon, England. He married Charity Martin on 25 May 1635, in Branscombe, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He was buried in Branscombe, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: William Perry est né en 1612, à Branscombe, Devon, Angleterre. Il a épousé Charity Martin le 25 mai 1635, à Branscombe, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Ilenterré à Branscombe, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: William Perry wurde 1612 in Branscombe, Devon, England geboren. Er hat am 25. Mai 1635 in Branscombe, Devon, England Charity Martin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er wurde in Branscombe, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: William Perry è nato il nel 1612 nel Branscombe, Devon, England. Lui sposato Charity Martin il 25 maggio 1635, nel Branscombe, Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Fu sepolto a Branscombe, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Perryは1612年にイギリスデボンブランスコンベで生まれた。1635年5月25日にイギリスデボンブランスコンベでCharity Martinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスデボンブランスコンベにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Perry(은)는 1612년 영국 데폰 브랜스코브에서 태어났다. 그는 1635년 5월 25일 영국 데폰 브랜스코브에서 Charity Martin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 데폰 브랜스코브에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: William Perry родился в 1612 г. в местности бранскомб, девон, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Charity Martin 25 мая 1635 г. в местности бранскомб, девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он был похоронен в местности бранскомб, девон, Англия.
